Hero.title: Flos Ferri
Hero.motto: Historical reenactment, medieval fencing and Renaissance dances.

About.title: The Company
About.subtitle: The Company
About.p1: The Flos Ferri association was founded in 2001 by Gianluca Falcone and Marco Signorini, who, after some years of solitary practice of the art of Medieval Fencing, decided to share their passion with anyone interested in this discipline.
About.p2: The group mainly deals with the reconstruction of fencing techniques, studied through historical documents and manuals that treat the art of war in the period from the end of the 13th century to the beginning of the 15th century, with particular attention to the Italian school of the famous magistro <wiki>Fiore Dei Liberi</wiki>.
About.p3: Combat practice, the backbone of Flos Ferri, is divided between training and public performances. The latter are completed through historical reconstruction and dissemination activities that revolve around the camp and are functional and vital to our group. Besides being rewarding and fun for us, and bringing many young people closer to culture and history through the exercise of fencing, combat is an opportunity to arouse interest and attract the public into a short journey through time that can touch upon the most diverse aspects of medieval life.
About.p4: Moving with our camp, we have the opportunity to reenact battles and perform dances during festivals and ceremonies held in the villages of Italy. All our activities, as well as costumes, weapons, tools, and the camp itself, are reconstructed based on research conducted on sources of the time, university studies, treatises, and dedicated publications. All these efforts then materialise in the short space of a duel or a dance, in the long hours spent creating objects and accessories, and in the selection of the best reproductions of original artefacts to add to our collection.
About.p5: The audience only has to let themselves be transported on a journey through time, whether it is to find a few minutes of escape or to fill the most unusual curiosities. The important thing is that it is a moment of aggregation, sharing, and fun for everyone!
About.p6: Our medieval fencing training sessions take place at the <maps>Quake youth centre in via Eraclea n. 25 Ravenna</maps> every Friday from 9:00 PM to 11:00 PM, while dance dates are communicated monthly, with the possibility of moving to the <maps_rocca>Rocca of Ravenna</maps_rocca> in the summer months.

Fencing.title: Medieval Fencing
Fencing.subtitle: The Art of Arms
Fencing.intro: Born as a group of armed men, the main activity of Flos Ferri is the study of the techniques and martial arts of the period, ranging from the end of the 13th to the beginning of the 15th century. These have come down to us thanks to the work of great masters or anonymous people who immortalised on parchment the countless techniques for coming out alive from a battle, a duel, or, more simply, a street brawl.
Fencing.activity: The main treatises on which we base our fencing are the I33 and the Flos Duellatorum, in homage to whose author – <wiki>Fiore de’ Liberi</wiki> – the name of our company was born.
Fencing.research: Unfortunately, the documentation that has come down to us does not explain in detail how the men of the time fought, so this is where our reconstruction and research work begins. Through the analysis of the treatises, practice, and the study of weapons and armour, we try to understand what the principles were that formed the basis of a real martial art, which has nothing to envy the most celebrated Eastern techniques.
Fencing.vision: All this work, which proceeds year by year, materialises in the few minutes of a duel or in the most hectic phases of a pitched battle, giving us the satisfaction of having given our best and being rewarded by applause or the admiration of the spectators.
Fencing.public: We want to clarify that all our fights are not choreographed or agreed upon for mere spectacularity, but are the result of training that allows us to recreate realistic duels, while maintaining adequate levels of safety thanks to the mastery of the weapons used. Another aspect we focus on a lot in our performances is emphasizing how physical and instinctive the way of fighting of the man-at-arms of the 14th century was, compared to the 'civil' fencing that spread in the following centuries outside the lists and battlefields.

details.p1: For this reason, we easily pass from sword and buckler to the dagger, from the poleaxe to the longsword, even if the clashes often end in wrestling: grappling, takedowns, and locks, exactly as suggested by the miniatures of the Flos Duellatorum and other treatises intended for the warrior elite of the time.

Camp.title: The Camp
Camp.subtitle: Daily Life
Camp.p1: The camp is the hub around which all the group's activities rotate: it is therefore not surprising that it is continuously expanding or updating. Furthermore, being our home for the entire duration of the outings, it is easy to find it in disorder (contrary to what one might think, this is something we are proud of). In fact, the camp is not simply a background or a set, it is a real support for daily life and every part of it is designed with a purpose: the awnings protect us from the sun and light rain, the tents shield us from the humidity of the night and summer storms, the tables and benches become a canteen where we serve food prepared in our kitchen and a meeting point after a day of work, and finally, the racks collect weapons and armor so that they are always visible and accessible to the public. It is for this reason that our camp will never be perfect, but at the same time, it appears very real and welcoming, designed time after time to welcome the public and put them in a position to see, feel, and touch everything they desire with their own hands.

Dance.title: Renaissance Dances
Dance.subtitle: Dance & Arts
Dance.p1: Joyful and festive throughout the 14th century, fast and geometric in the 15th century, dancing between the Middle Ages and the Renaissance was not just a pastime or entertainment, but above all a courtship technique and a playful expression. In the geometric choreographies, in the rhythmic correspondence of music and steps, in the fine movements, there is all the poetry and concreteness of the Renaissance. From medieval manors to Renaissance palaces, the courtly and noble environment hosted refined social dances, the favourite pastime of the elite, who were always careful to keep up to date with the fashion of the time.
Dance.p2: Thus appeared the first manuals of the masters of the time such as 'De arte saltandi et choreaus ducendi' by Domenico da Piacenza (first dance master), 'De pratica seu arte tripudii vulgare opusculum' by Guglielmo Ebreo da Pesaro (the most important student of Domenico who with his explanations managed to make the dances of the time understood) and 'Libro dell’arte del danzare' by Antonio Cornazzano (also a student of Domenico, but in his text the description of the dances is precise although too synthetic).
Dance.p3: Dance parties (usually wedding banquets, ceremonies, coronations) began with the Carola in the 15th century, which alternated the forms of the circle and the chain, mimicking a ritual 'for flirting' with partners, who perform in these evolutions, stopping to look at each other and then starting again. With the advent of Humanism, dance was revalued to the point of being compared to an art form, a distinctive sign of the noble classes. Therefore, on the one hand, the Saltarello developed as a symbol of the explosion of popular dance, while the Bassadanza became the symbol of court ceremonies.
Dance.p4: Subsequently, in the 16th century in France, people danced through the 'branles' (dances in chains or in circles, with the introduction of skipped steps) which were usually used to open parties, because it gave the opportunity for the guests present to get to know each other, start talking, shaking hands and starting these simple dances.
Dance.p5: Finally, the group deals with English dances called 'English Country Dances' which were published in 1651 by J. Playford. The text collects almost a hundred folk dances that already in the previous century had 'invaded' the folk tradition thanks to their simplicity and playfulness.
Dance.p6: Each time the Flos Ferri association tries to transmit to the public, present its knowledge learned from the study of treatises by the masters of the time and invites anyone who wants to interact in the moments of animation that conclude the performances, dancing and having fun with them!

Contact.p1: Get in touch with us for info, courses or events.

Footer.runts: Registered in RUNTS (APS section) since 02/07/2024 - Repertory 139207
Footer.privacy_modal_intro: This page has been designed to be cookie-free. We do not use profiling cookies, and we do not collect users' personal data for commercial or tracking purposes. Site performance data is collected completely anonymously by Vercel for statistical purposes only. No browsing data is transferred to third parties.
Footer.privacy_modal_section_contacts_title: Processing of data sent voluntarily by the user (Contacts)
Footer.privacy_modal_section_contacts_intro: If you decide to contact us using the links on the site, we will process the data you spontaneously provide according to the principles of fairness and transparency:
Footer.privacy_modal_email_content: By clicking on the mail link, you will send us your email address and the information contained in the message. This data will be used exclusively to respond to your request and will not be shared with third parties.
Footer.privacy_modal_whatsapp_content: The site contains a direct link to the WhatsApp platform. By clicking the link, you will be redirected to Meta Platforms Ireland Limited's systems. Sending a message involves sharing your phone number and the name associated with your profile. We invite you to consult the WhatsApp Privacy Policy to understand how they manage your data. We will use your contact information only and exclusively to exchange messages related to your request.
Footer.privacy_modal_holder_content: For any questions or to exercise your rights on the data sent (e.g. requesting its deletion), you can write to us at: <email>info@flosferri.it</email>.

statute_modal.title: Statute
statute_modal.disclaimer: For everything not covered in this Statute, the provisions of decree 117/2017 and the Civil Code apply.

sections.art1_content: Based on the principle of subsidiarity referred to in art.118 fourth paragraph of the Constitution, in accordance with the Civil Code, Law 106/2016 and Legislative Decree 3 July 2017 n.117, a Social Promotion Association is established, which takes the name of "Flos Ferri APS". The acronym APS and the name Social Promotion Association can only be used if the Association is registered in the "Social Promotion Associations" section of the National Single Register of Third Sector Entities (RUNTS). The Association has its headquarters in the Municipality of Ravenna (Province of Ravenna). The change of headquarters within the same municipality can be resolved by the social assembly and does not involve any statutory modification.
sections.art2_intro: The Association is a non-commercial and non-profit entity, which operates with civic, solidarity and social utility purposes in order to promote historical reenactment and reconstruction, with particular reference to the study and dissemination of historical events, music and dances, arts and crafts, cooking, fashion, games and pastimes and martial arts. It also operates in compliance with constitutional and moral principles in order to:
art2_list: contribute to implementing articles 2, 3, 4 and 18 of the Constitution.
allow its members and citizens in general, through the activity carried out, civil and cultural growth, social cohesion and improvement of the quality of life.

support the autonomous initiative of those who contribute, even in associated form, to pursue the common good, to elevate the levels of active citizenship, cohesion and social protection, favouring participation, inclusion and the full development of the person.
valorise their potential for growth and job occupation.

particularly favour the participation of children, young people, women, the elderly, and citizens, anyway disadvantaged on a physical, social, cultural, or economic level, in the activities referred to in title II of this statute, operating to formally and concretely protect their rights, in particular as users and participants in the activities organised by the Association.
sections.art2_footer: Within the scope of the purposes and general principles, it mainly carries out, in favour of its members, their cohabiting family members and third parties, the activities of general interest provided for by this statute, in the form of voluntary action, or mutuality, or free provision of services or production or exchange of goods and services. The association, as it is not recognised, is also regulated by articles 36 and following of the civil code.
sections.art3_content: The duration of the Association is unlimited. It can only be dissolved by resolution of the extraordinary assembly of members, in the manner provided for in this statute.
sections.art4_title: Art. 4 The activities of the Association
sections.art4_intro: The Association exercises and organises the following activities, in accordance with the provisions of articles 5, 6 and 7 of legislative decree 117/2017, this statute and current legislation, also in collaboration with other Third Sector Entities or other public and private subjects and also through the management of plants, structures and premises:
art4_list: Secondary and instrumental activities to the institutional activity of general interest.
sections.art5_intro: To achieve its purposes, the Association exercises and organises primarily and in a prevalent way, in accordance with the particular rules governing its exercise, the following activities of general interest referred to in art. 5 of legislative decree 117/2017, with particular reference to the letters:
art5_list: education, instruction and vocational training, pursuant to the law of 28 March 2003, n. 53, and subsequent amendments, as well as cultural activities of social interest with educational purposes;
interventions for the protection and enhancement of cultural heritage and the landscape, pursuant to the legislative decree of 22 January 2004, n. 42, and subsequent amendments;

organisation and management of cultural, artistic or recreational activities of social interest, including activities, including publishing, for the promotion and dissemination of culture and the practice of volunteering and activities of general interest referred to in this article;
organisation and management of tourism activities of social, cultural or religious interest
organisation and management of amateur sports activities
requalification of unused public assets or assets confiscated from organised crime.
sections.art6_title: Art. 6 Secondary and instrumental activities to the institutional activities of general interest
sections.art6_intro: To achieve its purposes, and in order to finance its activities of general interest, the Association may also exercise and organize, pursuant to art. 6 of legislative decree 117/2017, further activities, secondary and instrumental to the activities of general interest referred to in this statute, according to criteria and limits defined by decree of the Ministry of Labor and Social Policies 19 May 2021 n.107. In particular, it can carry out:
art6_list: commercial activities strictly functional to achieving the statutory purposes and any other activity connected and functional to achieving the associative purposes permitted to non-profit entities by current legislative provisions. The identification of these activities is delegated to the association's Board of Directors.

The administration of food and drinks to the public on the occasion of particular events or manifestations, for the period of their development, in the premises and/or spaces where such manifestations take place, under the conditions provided for by art. 70 of legislative decree 117/2017;

as an AICS affiliate, an Entity included among those referred to in article 3, comma 6, letter e), of the law of 25 August 1991, n. 287, registered in the appropriate register, whose welfare purposes are recognized by the Ministry of the Interior, the administration of food and drinks to its members, its associates and their cohabiting family members, as well as to other social promotion associations that carry out the same activity and which by law, regulation, deed of incorporation or statute are part of a single local or national organization, of their respective associates or members and of those registered by the respective national organizations, at the sites where the institutional activity is carried out, in exchange for specific fees, according to current legislation.
sections.art7_content: To achieve its purposes, and in order to finance its activities of general interest, the association may also carry out fundraising activities, according to the provisions of art. 7 and 79 of legislative decree 117/2017, through the request to third parties for donations, bequests and contributions of a non-remunerative nature. Fundraising activity can be carried out both occasionally, also through offers of goods of modest value or services to supporters, in conjunction with celebrations, anniversaries or awareness campaigns, and in an organized and continuous form, also through solicitation to the public or through the transfer or provision of goods or services of modest value, using its own resources and those of third parties, including volunteers and employees, in compliance with the principles of truth, transparency and fairness in relations with supporters and the public, according to the Guidelines adopted by Decree of the Ministry of Labor and Social Policies 9 June 2022 and subsequent amendments and integrations.
sections.art8_title: Art. 8 Management and methods of carrying out the organised activities
sections.art8_content: For the realisation of its activities, for the management of the territory, at all levels, of projects in the field of social association, for the realisation of specific objectives, for the direct management of certain services, the Association can collaborate with other entities of the third sector and with non-profit entities, as well as with public and private subjects. It can also stipulate agreements and conventions with them, and promote/or constitute/or adhere to, and/or collaborate with Associations, Institutes, Foundations, Cooperatives, Social Enterprises, and/or other instrumental entities without profit purposes. For the management and methods of carrying out activities of general interest, it privileges contributions based on personal, spontaneous, voluntary, and free performances, without profit purposes, not even indirect, and makes prevalent use of the voluntary activity of its members or of people belonging to the associated entities. When this is necessary for the purposes of pursuing its purposes and carrying out its activities, within the limits of what is provided for by the law. 36 of legislative decree 117/2017, the Association may use self-employed or other nature work, or subordinate work, or other forms of collaboration permitted by law, even of its own members.
sections.art9_content: Volunteers are people who, by their own free choice, carry out, through the association, activities in favour of the community and the common good, making their own time and abilities available. Their qualification is incompatible with any form of subordinate or self-employed work relationship, as well as with any other paid work relationship with the association. Their activity cannot be paid in any way, not even by the beneficiary. Volunteers can be reimbursed only for expenses actually incurred and documented for the activity performed, within the maximum limits and under the conditions previously established by the Board of Directors of the Association, also in accordance with the criteria referred to in article 17, comma 4, of legislative decree 117/2017. In any case, flat-rate expense reimbursements are prohibited. According to the provisions of articles 17 and 18 of the d.lgs. 117/2017, in the manner referred to in the decree of the Ministry of Economic Development 6 October 2021 and subsequent amendments and integrations, all volunteers must be insured against illnesses and accidents connected with the performance of the voluntary activity, as well as for civil liability towards third parties, and those who carry out their activity in a non-occasional way must also be registered in an appropriate register.
sections.art10_title: Art. 10 Social Year, Financial Statements, Accounting Records, Social Balance Sheet
sections.art10_content: The social year runs from 1 January to 31 December of each year. Each year, the Board of Directors prepares the financial statements. If the Association has achieved revenues of less than 220,000.00 euros, the budget can be prepared as a cash report. The financial statements must be approved by the social Assembly by 30 April of each year and deposited with the single register of the third sector by 30 June. If revenues exceed 1 million euros, a social report must also be drawn up.
sections.art11_content: The financial statements and the social balance sheet must be posted at the association's headquarters, and transmitted to all members entitled to vote or publicised through the social site. If the revenues are higher than 100,000.00 euros, any emoluments to members of the administration and control bodies must be published on the association's website.
sections.art12_content: The Association's assets consist of owned movable and immovable property, surpluses from exercises, donations, disbursements and bequests. The assets are used to perform the statutory activity. The distribution, even indirect, of profits and management surpluses to founders, associates, workers and administrators is prohibited.
sections.art13_content: Sources of funding include annual fees, proceeds from property management, proceeds from activities, public and private contributions, conventions and liberal disbursements.
sections.art14_title: Art. 14 Social books and Register of Volunteers
sections.art14_content: The Association keeps the books of the members, of the meetings of the Assembly and of the Board of Directors, as well as the register of volunteers. All members in good standing have the right to examine the social books.
sections.art15_content: The association has no limitations on the admission of members. All natural persons and Third Sector Entities that share its principles can join. To join, a written request must be made to the Board of Directors. Acceptance gives the right to the membership card. The fee is neither refundable nor transferable.
sections.art16_content: Members have the right to participate in activities, assemblies, approve budgets and statutes, and elect social bodies. Free eligibility for administrative bodies is guaranteed according to the principle of a single vote.
sections.art17_content: Members are required to support the purposes of the Association, observe the statute and pay the established social fees.
sections.art18_content: The qualification is lost through resignation, dissolution of the Association, death, exclusion for arrears or expulsion for serious infractions. The Board of Directors is competent with reasoned deliberations.
sections.title5: Title V - Bodies of the Association
sections.art19_content: They are Bodies of the Association: The Social Assembly, the Board of Directors and the President.
sections.art20_content: It is the highest body of the Association. It approves the budget, appoints the members of the social bodies and resolves on statutory changes. Each member has the right to one vote and can hold one proxy.
sections.art21_content: Elected by the Assembly, it is composed of 3 to 9 members. It remains in office for 4 years. It implements the directives of the Assembly and approves the activity programs.
sections.art22_content: Elected by the Assembly, he has the legal representation of the Association. He remains in office for 4 years and is re-eligible.
sections.art23_content: Mandatorily appointed when legal limits are exceeded or by the will of the Assembly. It supervises compliance with the law and the statute.
sections.title6: Title VI - Miscellaneous and final provisions
sections.art26_content: The amendments are approved by the Assembly with the favourable vote of at least two-thirds of those present, according to the prescribed constitutive quorums.
sections.art28_title: Art. 28 Dissolution and devolution of assets
sections.art28_content: For dissolution, the favourable vote of at least three-quarters of the members is required. The residual assets are devolved to other Third Sector Entities.
